1	package bead
2	
3	import "time"
4	
5	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.
6	// The schema matches bd/br JSONL format for interchange compatibility.
7	// Unknown fields from external sources are preserved in Extra.
8	type Bead struct {
9		ID        string    `json:"id"`
10		Title     string    `json:"title"`
11		Status    string    `json:"status"`
12		Priority  int       `json:"priority"`
13		IssueType string    `json:"issue_type"`
14		Owner     string    `json:"owner,omitempty"`
15		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
16		CreatedBy string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
17		UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
18	
19		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)
20		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
21		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
22		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
23		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
24		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
25	
26		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
27		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
28	
29		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.
30		// Workflow-specific fields (e.g. HELIX spec-id, execution-eligible)
31		// are stored here and written back on save.
32		Extra map[string]any `json:"-"`
33	}
34	
35	// Dependency represents a link between two beads (bd-compatible format).
36	type Dependency struct {
37		IssueID     string `json:"issue_id"`
38		DependsOnID string `json:"depends_on_id"`
39		Type        string `json:"type"` // "blocks", "related", etc.
40		CreatedAt   string `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
41		CreatedBy   string `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
42		Metadata    string `json:"metadata,omitempty"`
43	}
44	
45	// BeadEvent records append-only execution evidence.
46	type BeadEvent struct {
47		Kind      string    `json:"kind"`
48		Summary   string    `json:"summary,omitempty"`
49		Body      string    `json:"body,omitempty"`
50		Actor     string    `json:"actor,omitempty"`
51		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
52		Source    string    `json:"source,omitempty"`
53	}
54	
55	// Status constants
56	const (
57		StatusOpen       = "open"
58		StatusInProgress = "in_progress"
59		StatusClosed     = "closed"
60		// StatusBlocked marks a bead that is not eligible for agent dispatch —

1	package escalation

3	import (
4		"encoding/json"

8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"

11	// SuccessStatus mirrors agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess. The agent package's
12	// TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab guard catches drift if either side
13	// renames. Defined locally so escalation does not import agent.
14	const SuccessStatus = "success"
15	
16	// EscalatableStatuses is the set of executor status strings that warrant
17	// retrying with a higher tier. Mirrors a subset of agent.ExecuteBeadStatus*
18	// strings; the agent package has a TestEscalatableStatusesMatchAgentVocab
19	// guard (see agent/tier_escalation_alignment_test.go) to catch drift.
20	var EscalatableStatuses = map[string]bool{
21		"execution_failed":             true,
22		"post_run_check_failed":        true,
23		"land_conflict":                true,
24		"structural_validation_failed": true,
25	}
26	
27	// ShouldEscalate reports whether status warrants escalating to the next tier.
28	// Structural failures (e.g. validation errors) do not escalate because a
29	// smarter model cannot fix a malformed prompt or corrupted bead state.
30	func ShouldEscalate(status string) bool {
31		return EscalatableStatuses[status]
32	}
33	
34	// ProviderCooldownDuration is how long an unhealthy harness is skipped before
35	// re-probing. Five minutes gives most transient errors time to resolve without
36	// locking out a provider for too long.
37	//
38	// Per-harness cooldown tracking moved to the upstream agent service in v0.8.0:
39	// callers record failures via svc.RecordRouteAttempt and read cooldown state
40	// via svc.RouteStatus's RouteCandidateStatus.Healthy (ddx-7bc0c8d5).
41	const ProviderCooldownDuration = 5 * time.Minute
42

45		body := fmt.Sprintf("tier=%s harness=%s model=%s", tier, harness, model)

47			body += " probe=" + probeResult
48		}
49		if detail != "" {
50			body += "\n" + detail

1	package agent
2	
3	import "strings"
4	
5	const DefaultRoutingProfile = "default"
6	
7	// NormalizeRoutingProfile returns "default" for empty/whitespace-only strings,
8	// and the trimmed input otherwise.
9	func NormalizeRoutingProfile(profile string) string {
10		profile = strings.TrimSpace(profile)
11		if profile == "" {
12			return DefaultRoutingProfile
13		}
14		return profile
15	}

input.command:
git commit -m "feat(routing): metadata-driven tier inference replaces always-cheap default [ddx-b2c9a245]

Adds escalation.InferTier(*bead.Bead) ModelTier which inspects explicit
tier: labels, kind/priority labels, IssueType, and description scope to
recommend smart/standard/cheap. The execute-loop's zero-config
auto-route fallback now invokes this engine per bead instead of
hard-coding profile=cheap, aligning with the endpoint-first routing
redesign goal of cheap-by-default with metadata-driven escalation."

[detached HEAD 3ddf3d52] feat(routing): metadata-driven tier inference replaces always-cheap default [ddx-b2c9a245]

data.text:
Committed (3ddf3d52). Bead complete.

- `escalation.InferTier(*bead.Bead) ModelTier` reads explicit `tier:` labels first, then `priority:` (critical/urgent → smart, high+bug → smart, high → standard, low → cheap), then kind labels / IssueType (bug → standard, chore/docs → cheap, enhancement/feature → standard), then falls back to description+acceptance length (≥4000 → smart, ≥1500 → standard, else cheap). Defaults to cheap when nothing else matches, preserving the prior hot-fix behavior.
- `escalation.TierToProfile` maps the inferred tier to the existing routing-profile string.
- `cmd/agent_cmd.go runAgentExecuteLoopImpl`: replaces `profile = "cheap"` with `autoInferTier`. The executor closure loads each bead via `store.Get`, calls `InferTier`, and passes the tier through `singleTierAttempt`, which now derives the per-attempt profile from the tier when auto-infer is active.
- Tests in `cli/internal/escalation/tierinfer_test.go` cover nil bead, explicit tier label (and override over priority), priorities (critical/high+bug/high+enhancement/low), kind labels, IssueType fallback, scope thresholds, and the tier→profile map.
